name: Port of Bujaruelo – trail running from Bujaruelo
name_original: Puerto de Bujaruelo – trail running desde Bujaruelo
tips:
From the French side of Gavernie, a fairly well-paved slope winds up to the 2270-meter-high Port de Boucharo / Gavernie. With a steady gradient of 8-10%, the route is easy to drive and you can enjoy great views of the mountains of the famous Cirque de Gavernie along the way.
Unfortunately, there is only one hiking path on the Spanish side that could lead you down into the valley - with touring bikes only to be overcome as a sliding section.

After crossing the 13th century Romanesque bridge and before reaching the refuge of the same name, the remains of this hermitage with a semicircular floor plan are found. This is a popular starting point for routes to the valleys of Panticosa, Gavarnie, Ordesa and the smaller Otal and Ordiso.

waypoints.name: San Nicolás de Bujaruelo Stone Bridge and Hermitage
